summary: First-line treatment for high-risk metastatic breast cancer favors combination chemotherapy over single-agent therapies such as mitoxantrone due to superior efficacy and improved outcomes. There is insufficient evidence supporting equal effectiveness between single-agent mitoxantrone and combination regimens in this setting.
